studies.title: Evaluation of FAVAS ( Focal ambient visual acuity stimulation) digital based health application
studies.brief_summary: Amblyopia is a common disorder and a major cause of visual impairment in children. Abnormal visual stimulation during the critical period of visual development results in brain damage. Structural and functional damage occurs in the cortex of the visual center in the occipital lobe in the form of atrophy of connections and the eye becomes amblyopic. The Therapy of Amblyopia is currently the occlusion therapy of the good eye (after binocular glasses correction). The aim of the study is to optimize amblyopia therapy by evaluating the Course of treatment with application of the online Caterna vision therapy, which is supplementary to the occlusion therapy being used. Caterna Vision Therapy should bring improvement by active stimulation and achieve success to amblyopic patients. The therapy games of the Caterna vision training played on the computer at home for 30-45 minutes a day during the occlusion period for over 90 days.

classification.reason: The study aims to evaluate a specific intervention (Caterna Vision Therapy) for amblyopia, suggesting an interventional design. Preliminary analysis indicates it might be an RCT, and the description implies a prospective evaluation of treatment. However, the lack of explicit mention of randomization or control groups reduces confidence slightly.
